step_by_step_thinking:
Connective tissue width around an implant refers to the peri-implant soft tissue, particularly the keratinized mucosa. According to current standards and practices in dental implantology, maintaining adequate keratinization reduces the risk of microbial infiltration and subsequent infections. General guidelines suggest a desirable connective tissue width around an implant ranging from 2-3mm to ensure proper seal against bacteria and facilitate healthy integration. Given typical clinical protocols aiming to maintain optimal conditions around the implant, option B (\boxed{B}) aligns well with established recommendations.

According to current standards and practices in dental implantology, maintaining adequate keratinization reduces the risk of microbial infiltration and subsequent infections. General guidelines suggest a desirable connective tissue width around an implant ranging from 2-3mm to ensure proper seal against bacteria and facilitate healthy integration.
